Title: Finalize V1 control-plane and runtime boundary
Description: Lock the shared control plane vs dedicated runtime plane boundary for V1 and turn the trust assumptions into implementation rules. Acceptance: one environment model, one isolation policy, and one deployment ownership model are agreed and documented.

Title: Define the core Ruh.ai data model
Description: Define stable V1 entities for tenant, runtime environment, workspace, employee template, employee instance, work item, task, artifact, approval, and deployment. Acceptance: schema set supports platform services and pack contracts without vertical-specific fields.

Title: Build the template and pack registry
Description: Implement versioned storage and retrieval for employee templates, packs, workflow templates, policy profiles, and connector presets. Acceptance: packs can be published, versioned, and resolved by the deployment compiler.

Title: Implement the deployment compiler
Description: Build the deterministic compiler that renders pack plus overlay plus policy inputs into deployable OpenClaw runtime bundles. Acceptance: dry-run preview, compatibility checks, manifest output, and rollback bundle generation all work.

Title: Implement the runtime manager
Description: Track gateway health, applied bundle state, runtime inventory, drift, and deployment status per trust boundary. Acceptance: deployments can be applied, observed, and rolled back without manual state reconstruction.

Title: Implement the workflow orchestrator
Description: Model work as typed task graphs with explicit inputs, outputs, retries, deadlines, assignments, and escalations. Acceptance: the orchestrator advances workflow state without relying on transcripts as business state.

Title: Implement the artifact service
Description: Store structured outputs, lineage, receipts, and cross-workflow references for every employee result. Acceptance: artifacts can be versioned, linked to work items, and replayed during audits and evals.

Title: Implement the policy and approval service
Description: Centralize risk tiers, approval routing, override logging, and protected-action enforcement for V1. Acceptance: medium and high-risk actions are blocked unless policy allows them or a named approver authorizes them.

Title: Implement the secrets broker
Description: Introduce scoped secret references, short-lived leases, and audit logs for connector and runtime credentials. Acceptance: no pack or overlay requires raw credentials in source control or workspace files.

Title: Implement observability and eval scoring
Description: Capture run telemetry, quality samples, cost data, and eval outcomes in one service. Acceptance: the team can measure completion rate, approval quality, failure rate, and cost per workflow by pack and tenant.

Title: Publish the AI BuildOps pack contract
Description: Create the pack manifest, directory contract, exported assets, and compatibility rules for the AI BuildOps reference pack. Acceptance: the pack can be compiled without tenant-specific edits.

Title: Author AI BuildOps employee templates
Description: Define the seven default employee roles for intake, spec, planning, build orchestration, QA and evals, release, and incident feedback. Acceptance: each template has a narrow responsibility, tool profile, and output contract.

Title: Author AI BuildOps workflows and artifacts
Description: Define the canonical workflows and artifact schemas for intake, spec generation, planning, build orchestration, eval gating, release prep, and incident learning. Acceptance: every workflow has typed task contracts and output artifacts.

Title: Implement the GitHub + Linear + Slack + Sentry overlay
Description: Bind the reusable AI BuildOps pack to the target engineering stack with tenant-specific mappings, secret refs, and policy defaults. Acceptance: service to repo to team to channel to project routing is defined end-to-end.

Title: Define AI BuildOps eval profiles
Description: Create the eval suites and release gates needed for spec quality, build quality, protected-branch compliance, and incident-to-regression learning. Acceptance: every risky path has a measurable pass or fail gate.

Title: Pilot intake-to-spec and spec-to-build for one service
Description: Run the first narrow AI BuildOps slice on one product or service boundary before broader rollout. Acceptance: a work item can move from intake to approved spec to PR orchestration with human gates intact.

Title: Publish the Construction Project Ops pack contract
Description: Create the pack manifest, directory contract, exported assets, and compatibility rules for the construction operations pack. Acceptance: the pack is reusable across contractors without cloning workflow source.

Title: Author construction employee templates
Description: Define the intake, document control, RFI, submittal, meeting and reporting, and action tracking employee templates. Acceptance: each template has explicit citations, approval rules, and scoped actions.

Title: Author construction workflows and artifacts
Description: Define correspondence intake, RFI lifecycle, submittal lifecycle, meeting pack, reporting, and issue or risk watchlist workflows with typed artifacts. Acceptance: every workflow preserves project-scoped context and citation-first drafting.

Title: Implement the Procore + SharePoint + Teams overlay
Description: Bind the construction pack to the target document, PMIS, and communication stack with customer-specific mappings and approval channels. Acceptance: external writes stay gated and project-scoped routing is explicit.

Title: Define construction eval gates
Description: Add citation quality, routing correctness, stale-document detection, and approval compliance evals before production promotion. Acceptance: the pack can prove safe drafting behavior and detect out-of-date document use.

Title: Create the design-partner qualification scorecard
Description: Turn the pilot entry criteria into a repeatable checklist with required gates for sponsor, workflow owner, integrations, approval participants, trust boundary, and baseline metrics. Acceptance: unqualified pilot candidates are filtered out early.

Title: Create the baseline capture kit
Description: Define how to collect workflow maps, task samples, manual cycle time, effort baselines, and evidence artifacts before automation is enabled. Acceptance: every pilot starts with a locked before-state that can be measured against.

Title: Define the approval matrix and protected-action policy
Description: Map low, medium, and high-risk actions to named human approvers and default system behavior. Acceptance: merge, deploy, external communication, and other protected actions are never ambiguous during pilot rollout.

Title: Build the phased rollout runbook
Description: Define the move from monitor mode to draft mode to approval mode to constrained autonomy for each pilot. Acceptance: promotion criteria, rollback conditions, and operator expectations are explicit for every phase.

Title: Create the weekly scorecard and gate review cadence
Description: Define the operating ritual for reviewing value, trust, technical health, and adoption every week. Acceptance: each pilot has one scorecard, one owner, and one go or no-go decision point per phase.

Title: Run connector least-privilege and health checks
Description: Verify that every pilot connector has the minimum required access, healthy auth, and one explicit test path before live usage. Acceptance: GitHub or PMIS, Linear, Slack or Teams, and Sentry or document systems pass preflight checks.

Title: Create the pilot findings feedback loop
Description: Turn every exception, false positive, missing permission, and operator complaint into a reusable pack, overlay, or platform issue. Acceptance: pilot learnings are captured as backlog items instead of staying in chat or meetings.
